top_stories.title: Cisco Patches Four Critical Identity Services, Webex Flaws Enabling Code Execution
top_stories.summary: Cisco has released patches for four critical vulnerabilities affecting its Identity Services Engine (ISE) and Webex, with CVSS scores up to 9.8, enabling arbitrary code execution and user impersonation. One flaw (CVE-2026-20184) involves improper certificate validation in SSO integration, directly impacting authentication and identity infrastructure.

top_stories.title: Cross‑tenant helpdesk impersonation to data exfiltration: A human-operated intrusion playbook
top_stories.summary: Threat actors are conducting social engineering attacks via Microsoft Teams by impersonating IT helpdesk staff to trick users into granting remote access across tenant boundaries. The attack chain leverages legitimate administrative tools and standard protocols for lateral movement and data exfiltration, exploiting cross-tenant collaboration features in Microsoft's platform.

top_stories.title: Vishing attacks on Okta identity systems on the rise
top_stories.summary: LevelBlue researchers published findings on April 13 detailing a rise in vishing attacks targeting Okta identity systems. Attackers call victims or IT help desks directly to socially engineer them into weakening or resetting MFA protections on Okta accounts.

top_stories.title: Vercel Employee's AI Tool Access Led to Data Breach
top_stories.summary: A Vercel employee's access to an AI tool resulted in a data breach via stolen OAuth tokens. Security researchers highlight OAuth tokens as an emerging primary attack surface and vector for lateral movement in modern breaches.

top_stories.title: Tycoon 2FA Phishers Scatter, Adopt Device Code Phishing
top_stories.summary: Threat actors behind the Tycoon 2FA phishing-as-a-service platform have shifted tactics to adopt device code phishing attacks. The technique exploits legitimate OAuth device authorization flows to bypass MFA protections, tricking victims into granting attackers persistent account access tokens.

news.title: Microsoft: Teams increasingly abused in helpdesk impersonation attacks
news.summary: Microsoft has issued a warning about threat actors increasingly abusing Microsoft Teams through helpdesk impersonation attacks to gain access to enterprise networks. The attacks leverage legitimate tools for access and lateral movement, exploiting Teams' external collaboration features to bypass traditional security controls.

news.title: Vercel breached via compromised third-party AI tool
news.summary: Vercel suffered a security breach originating from a compromise of Context.ai, a third-party AI tool used by a Vercel employee. The attacker leveraged that access to take over the employee's Vercel Google account, ultimately compromising credentials of a limited subset of Vercel customers.

news.title: AI platform ATHR makes voice phishing a one-person job
news.summary: A criminal AI platform called ATHR enables automated voice-phishing operations for $4,000, spoofing email alerts from Google, Microsoft, and Coinbase to trick victims into calling back and engaging with AI voice agents or human scammers. The platform represents a commoditization of vishing attacks that directly target credential theft and account takeover, core concerns for IAM professionals.

news.title: Vercel Breach Tied to Context AI Hack Exposes Limited Customer Credentials
news.summary: Vercel disclosed a security breach originating from a compromised third-party AI tool (Context.ai), which led to unauthorized access to internal systems and a Vercel employee's Google Workspace account. The incident highlights supply chain and third-party access risks, with attackers leveraging compromised credentials to pivot into internal systems.

news.title: [Webinar] Eliminate Ghost Identities Before They Expose Your Enterprise Data
news.summary: A webinar is being promoted on the topic of non-human identity (NHI) management, focusing on unmanaged service accounts, API tokens, and OAuth grants as security risks. The session addresses the lifecycle management of automated credentials, citing that compromised service accounts and forgotten API keys accounted for 68% of cloud breaches in 2024.

news.title: Three Microsoft Defender Zero-Days Actively Exploited; Two Still Unpatched
news.summary: Huntress is warning that threat actors are actively exploiting three zero-day vulnerabilities in Microsoft Defender, tracked as BlueHammer, RedSun, and UnDefend, to gain elevated privileges on compromised systems. Two of the three vulnerabilities remain unpatched, with the flaws disclosed by a researcher known as Chaotic Eclipse.

news.title: Sam Altman's project World looks to scale its human verification empire. First stop: Tinder. | TechCrunch
news.summary: Sam Altman's World project (formerly Worldcoin) is expanding its biometric human verification system through new partnerships, with Tinder announced as an early partner. The project uses its iris-scanning Orb device to create anonymous digital identity credentials that verify users are human, positioning it as an identity verification layer for online platforms.

news.title: Sam Altman's World Teams With Zoom, Tinder to Better Verify Humans in the AI Age
news.summary: Sam Altman's World (formerly Worldcoin) is expanding its iris-scanning biometric identity verification system through new partnerships with Tinder for U.S. human verification and Zoom's Deep Face feature. The integrations use World's biometric proof-of-humanity technology to distinguish real humans from AI-generated entities in digital interactions.

news.title: Sam Altman’s World project launches major upgrade to fight deepfakes and bots
news.summary: Sam Altman's World project has launched a major upgrade aimed at combating deepfakes and bots through biometric identity verification. The project uses iris-scanning technology to establish proof-of-human identity in an effort to distinguish real users from AI-generated entities.

news.title: Sam Altman’s World releases major protocol upgrade, introduces World ID app
news.summary: Sam Altman's World project has released a major protocol upgrade and introduced a dedicated World ID app. The update focuses on digital identity verification and human authenticity confirmation, targeting identity security in AI-driven environments.

news.title: Microsoft's April patch puts Windows domain controllers into reboot loops — third known issue from KB5082063 is affecting Windows Server 2016 through 2025
news.summary: Microsoft's April patch KB5082063 is causing continuous reboot loops on Windows Server domain controllers running versions 2016 through 2025. This is the third known issue identified from this update, directly impacting enterprise Active Directory infrastructure critical to identity and authentication services.

news.title: Two-Factor Authentication Breaks Free From the Desktop
news.summary: The article discusses extending two-factor authentication (2FA) beyond traditional desktop/IT environments to address physical world security scenarios. The piece focuses on how threat actors bypass security systems outside standard IT environments and proposes 2FA as an additional barrier.

news.title: SailPoint、AWSとの戦略的協業契約を締結　ユニファイド アイデンティティ ガバナンス レイヤーによるAIエージェントの保護を可能に
news.summary: SailPoint announced a strategic collaboration agreement with AWS to enable protection of AI agents through a unified identity governance layer. The partnership aims to extend SailPoint's identity governance capabilities to secure agentic AI workloads running on AWS infrastructure.

news.title: Microsoft, Salesforce Patch AI Agent Data Leak Flaws
news.summary: Microsoft and Salesforce patched prompt injection vulnerabilities in their respective AI agent platforms, Copilot and Agentforce, that could have allowed external attackers to exfiltrate sensitive data. The flaws relate to AI agent security rather than core IAM functionality, though they touch on authorization boundaries within AI-assisted enterprise tools.

news.title: Privilege Elevation Dominates Massive Microsoft Patch Update
news.summary: Microsoft released a massive patch update addressing 165 vulnerabilities, with privilege elevation bugs accounting for more than half of the total. Two zero-day vulnerabilities were included among the elevation-of-privilege flaws patched in the update.

news.title: APT41 Delivers 'Zero-Detection' Backdoor to Harvest Cloud Credentials
news.summary: APT41, a China-backed threat group, has deployed a "zero-detection" backdoor specifically targeting cloud credentials across AWS, Google, Azure, and Alibaba environments. The group uses typosquatting techniques to obscure command-and-control communication while harvesting cloud identity credentials.

news.title: Your Next Breach Will Look Like Business as Usual
news.summary: The article addresses the growing challenge of detecting credential-based attacks, which increasingly blend in with normal business activity. It outlines detection model shifts cybersecurity teams need to adopt to counter identity-based threats.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence hackers exploited vulnerabilities in older internet routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from over 18,000 networks. The campaign required no malware deployment, relying entirely on stolen authentication tokens to gain unauthorized access.

news.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
news.summary: A new phishing-as-a-service platform called "Starkiller" proxies real login pages in real-time, relaying victims' credentials and MFA codes to legitimate sites rather than using static page copies. The service acts as a man-in-the-middle relay, effectively bypassing MFA protections and evading traditional phishing detection methods.
